revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇}}, {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇}}, Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県}}. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造}} to the east and {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|ja|下里古墳}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇}}, achieving the rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Nanbokuchō period|ja|南北朝時代}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>
== References ==
{{reflist}} == See Also == {{Col| * [[Kumano Province]] * [[Muro District]] * [[Kinan]] | * [[Nigihayahi]] * [[Kusunoki clan]] * [[Southern Court (Japan)]] }}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

'''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=draft}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=draft}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=draft}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=draft}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇|12=draft}}, Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=draft}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=draft}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|ja|下里古墳|12=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=draft}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, achieving the rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Nanbokuchō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=draft}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

'''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=draft}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=draft}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=draft}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=draft}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇|12=draft}}, Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=draft}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=draft}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|ja|下里古墳|12=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=draft}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, achieving the rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Nanbokuchō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=draft}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Kuni no Miyatsuko]] to [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀|12=draft}}'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|ja|成務天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=draft}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=draft}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|ja|新撰姓氏録|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku|12=draft}}'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=draft}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=draft}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=draft}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|ja|醍醐天皇|12=draft}}, Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=draft}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|ja|和歌山県|12=draft}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|ja|三重県|12=draft}}. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=draft}} to the east and {{ill|Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=draft}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=draft}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|ja|熊野速玉大社|12=draft}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|ja|下里古墳|12=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=draft}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=draft}}, achieving the rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Nanbokuchō period|ja|南北朝時代|12=draft}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=draft}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=draft}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== See Also == {{Col| * [[Kumano Province]] * [[Muro District]] * [[Kinan]] | * [[Nigihayahi]] * [[Kusunoki clan]] * [[Southern Court (Japan)]] }}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}

== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|ja|下里古墳|12=draft}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.

== References ==

revision.comment: Bot: single‑shot reformat + ill→|12=draft + draft categories + iws + DEFAULTSORT + move HTML comments + meta‑cat check
106 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:熊野国造 up to rev 101442115
revision.text:
{{日本の氏族 |家名 = 熊野国造 |家紋 = |家紋名称 = |本姓 = [[熊野氏]] |家祖 = [[大阿刀足尼]] |種別 = [[神別]]（[[天神]]） |出身地 = [[大和国|大倭国]] |根拠地 = 熊野国（のちの[[紀伊国]]） |人物 = [[#人物]]参照 |支流 = （括弧内は姓）
[[物部氏]]（連） }} '''熊野国造'''（くまののくにのみやつこ、くまのこくぞう）とは、[[#支配領域|熊野国]]（後の[[紀伊国]]南部）を支配した[[国造]]。 == 概要 == === 祖先 === *『[[先代旧事本紀]]』「国造本紀」に[[饒速日命]]の5世孫・[[大阿刀足尼]]が[[成務天皇]]の時代に熊野国造となったと伝わる。 *『[[新撰姓氏録]]』「山城（神別・天神）」には'''熊野連'''初代国造大阿刀足尼（おおあとのすくね）の子の稲比が「直」（あたえ）の姓を賜ったので本来は「熊野直」だが、その子孫に熊野本宮禰宜に任じた伍百枝がおり、伍百枝の孫の蝶のとき宗家は連（むらじ）を賜った。が饒速日命孫味饒田命之後也と見える。 === 氏族 === '''[[熊野氏]]'''（くまのうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[直 (姓)|直]]）。[[奈良時代]]には[[熊野広浜]]を輩出した。 「和田氏系図」によると、[[熊野国]]が[[紀伊国]]に編入されてからは[[牟婁郡]][[大領]]、また[[熊野本宮]][[禰宜]]の職に就き、代々本宮を奉斎したという。さらに「和田氏系図」によると、[[醍醐天皇]]の代に牟婁郡大領（[[従五位下]][[衛門府|右衛門尉]]）[[橘広方|熊野広方（橘広方）]]が'''[[橘氏|橘姓]]'''に改姓（[[橘良殖]]の[[猶子]]となったか）、広方の娘は[[鈴木良氏]]に嫁いで鈴木重氏の生母となった。広方の 3代後の橘良冬（和田良冬）が'''和田庄司'''を称した。 それ以来、国造家は和田庄司（和田国造）を称して[[和田氏 (曖昧さ回避)|和田氏]]となり、神仏習合の時代に中央の後盾をえた熊野三山社僧勢力の筆頭であった[[熊野別当]]の勢力におされたが、子孫は[[土豪]]として[[紀伊国]]全域、[[河内国]]などに勢力を持った。 == 本拠 == === 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''熊野国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[紀伊国]]'''[[牟婁郡]]'''、現在の[[和歌山県]]南部と[[三重県]]南部にあたる。東部は[[島津国造]]、北西部は[[紀国造]]と接している。 == 氏神 == [[紀伊国]][[牟婁郡]]の[[式内社]]である'''[[熊野本宮大社]]'''か。[[名神大社]]の格を持つ。 === 関連神社 === *[[熊野速玉大社]]（くまのはやたまたいしゃ） *:[[新宮市]]に鎮座する式内社。 === 墓 === *'''[[下里古墳]]'''（しもさとこふん） *:全長約40メートルの[[前方後円墳]]で、[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]初頭の築造。紀伊半島南部で唯一の前方後円墳とされる。 == 子孫 == * 『[[続日本紀]]』に、牟漏郡の[[采女]]・[[熊野広浜|熊野直広浜]]（ひろはま）が[[聖武天皇]]の代から[[孝謙天皇|称徳天皇]]の代まで（計4代）仕えたことが見える（[[769年|神護景雲3年]]没・[[従四位下]]）。 * [[平安時代]]には、分家の真砂庄司も「真砂の[[長者]]」と呼ばれて栄えた。[[清姫]]伝説では、主人公の女は真砂の庄司清次の娘（古い伝えでは清次の妻あるいは未亡人）とされる。[[真砂氏]]（滝尻王子社領）を『[[太平記]]』などに登場する「[[熊野八庄司]]」の一つとする説もある。 * [[南北朝時代_(日本)|南北朝時代]]の[[河内和田氏]]（[[楠木氏]]同族）を熊野国造の後裔とする説もある{{Sfn|太田亮|1935|pp=2144–2145}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Notelist}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{オープンアクセス}}{{Cite book|和書|author=太田亮|authorlink=太田亮|others=[[上田萬年]]、[[三上参次]]監修|chapter=熊野　クマノ　クマヌ　ユヤ|pages=2142-2152|volume=第2|date=1935|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/165}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|ncid=BN05000207|oclc=673726070|ref=harv}} ==関連項目== {{Col| * [[熊野国]] * [[牟婁郡]] * [[南紀]] | * [[ニギハヤヒ]] * [[楠木氏]] * [[南朝 (日本)|南朝]] }} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:くまののくにのみやつこ}} [[Category:三重県の歴史]] [[Category:和歌山県の歴史]] [[Category:楠木氏|+]] [[Category:紀伊国]] [[Category:紀伊国の氏族]] [[Category:物部氏|-くまののくにのみやつこ]] [[Category:国造]]

book|和書|author=太田亮|authorlink=太田亮|others=[[上田萬年]]、[[三上参次]]監修|chapter=熊野　クマノ　クマヌ　ユヤ|pages=2142-2152|volume=第2|date=1935|title=姓氏家系大辞典|publisher=姓氏家系大辞典刊行会|id={{全国書誌番号|47004572}}|url={{NDLDC|1130938/165}} 国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション|ncid=BN05000207|oclc=673726070|ref=harv}} ==関連項目== {{Col| * [[熊野国]] * [[牟婁郡]] * [[南紀]] | * [[ニギハヤヒ]] * [[楠木氏]] * [[南朝 (日本)|南朝]] }} {{Japanese-history-stub}} revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11568883]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|qq=|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|qq=|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|qq=|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|qq=|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|qq=|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>
== References ==
{{reflist}}
== See Also == {{Col| * [[Kumano Province]] * [[Muro District]] * [[Kinan]] | * [[Nigihayahi]] * [[Kusunoki clan]] * [[Southern Court (Japan)]] }}
{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|熊野国造|version=101442115|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:History of Mie Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Kusunoki clan]] [[Category:Kii Province]]
[[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
}}

=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|qq=|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.

It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.

=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|qq=|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|qq=|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|qq=|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11568883]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>
== References ==

It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11568883]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11568883]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>
== References ==

=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11568883]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11568883]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11568883]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:42, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]]'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Emperor Seimu]], {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''[[:en:Shinsen Shōjiroku|Shinsen Shōjiroku]]'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]]. During the reign of [[:en:Emperor Daigo|Emperor Daigo]], Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] and [[:en:Mie Prefecture|Mie Prefecture]]. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]], a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * [[:en:Kumano Hayatama Taisha|Kumano Hayatama Taisha]] (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the [[:en:Shimosato Kofun|Shimosato Kofun]], a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the [[:en:Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|Nanbokuchō period]] and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>
== References ==

=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.

It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.

=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)

'''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo}}, Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|en|Shimosato Kofun|WD=Q11361369|lt=Shimosato Kofun}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanbokuchō period}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)

During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo}}, Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture}}. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|en|Shimosato Kofun|WD=Q11361369|lt=Shimosato Kofun}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanbokuchō period}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Wada clan (Kawachi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:熊野国造]]
'''Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (熊野国造, Governor of Kumano) was the title of the ruler who governed the Kumano Province, which later became part of Kii Province in Japan.
== Overview == === Ancestry === * According to the ''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" section, during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Seimu|en|Emperor Seimu|WD=Q316672|lt=Emperor Seimu|1=Emperor Seimu}}, {{ill|Ōato Ashinai|ja|大阿刀足尼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōato Ashinai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a fifth-generation descendant of {{ill|Nigihayahi|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, was appointed as the Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko. * The ''{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|WD=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' under "Yamashiro (Divine Origin, Heavenly Deities)" lists the {{ill|Kumano Muraji|ja|熊野連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, noting their descent from Nigihayahi’s grandson Aji-Nigita-no-Mikoto.<ref>The original surname was likely "Kumano Ason," as indicated by historical notes about the lineage receiving the "Muraji" title later.</ref>
=== Clan === The {{ill|Kumano clan|ja|熊野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (熊野氏, surname: ''Na'') was a prominent clan. In the Nara period, notable figures such as {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} emerged from this lineage.
The "Wada Clan Genealogy" indicates that after the incorporation of Kumano Province into Kii Province, clan members served as ''daimyō'' of Muro District and hereditary priests of {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha|1=Kumano Hongū Taisha}}. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Daigo|en|Emperor Daigo|WD=Q329753|lt=Emperor Daigo|1=Emperor Daigo}}, Kumano no Hirokata, also known as {{ill|Tachibana no Hirokata|ja|橘広方|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Hirokata|1=Tachibana no Hirokata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20041240}}, adopted the Tachibana surname. His descendants later formed the Wada clan and played significant roles in local governance and religious affairs.
=== Governance === The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko governed Kumano Province, which corresponded to the southern region of modern-day {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}} and {{ill|Mie Prefecture|en|Mie Prefecture|WD=Q128196|lt=Mie Prefecture|1=Mie Prefecture}}. It was bordered by {{ill|Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|島津国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimazu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} to the east and {{ill|Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Kii no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|紀国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 紀伊国造}} to the northwest.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha|1=Kumano Hongū Taisha}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|en|Shimosato Kofun|WD=Q11361369|lt=Shimosato Kofun|1=Shimosato Kofun}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanbokuchō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Q65272223|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>

== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine was likely {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha|1=Kumano Hongū Taisha}}, a prominent Shinto shrine and one of the {{ill|Ninja Taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninja Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} in the region.
=== Related Shrines === * {{ill|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|en|Kumano Hayatama Taisha|WD=Q335618|lt=Kumano Hayatama Taisha|1=Kumano Hayatama Taisha}} (Shingū, Wakayama Prefecture)
== Burial Site == The clan’s burial site may include the {{ill|Shimosato Kofun|en|Shimosato Kofun|WD=Q11361369|lt=Shimosato Kofun|1=Shimosato Kofun}}, a keyhole-shaped tumulus dating to the late 4th or early 5th century, the only such tomb in the southern Kii Peninsula.
== Descendants == * {{ill|Kumano no Hirohama|ja|熊野広浜|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kumano no Hirohama|1=Kumano no Hirohama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109287645}} served as a court official during the reigns of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} through {{ill|Empress Shōtoku|ja|称徳天皇|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Shōtoku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 孝謙天皇|1=Empress Kōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q232955}}, achieving the Rank of Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, before his death in 769 CE. * During the Heian period, the clan produced notable figures such as the head of the Masago clan, who was known as the "Elder of Masago." * The Kumano no Kuni no Miyatsuko lineage is also linked to the {{ill|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|en|Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)|WD=Q8140349|lt=Nanbokuchō period|1=Northern and Southern Courts period (disambiguation)}} and figures such as the {{ill|Wada clan (Kawachi)|ja|河内和田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wada clan (Kawachi)|1=Q65272223|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q65272223}}, said to share descent with the {{ill|Kusunoki clan|ja|楠木氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kusunoki clan|1=Kusunoki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11541208}}.<ref>{{Cite book|author=Ōta Ryō|title=Sei Shi Kakei Daijiten|year=1935|volume=2|pages=2144–2145|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1130938/165}}</ref>
== References ==
